name: Sahelices del Río from Cea – circular by the river Cea and the Castillo
name_original: Sahelices del Río desde Cea – circular por el río Cea y el Castillo
region_intro: Hiking around Cea, a region in León, Spain, offers trails through diverse landscapes. The area is characterized by the presence of the río Cea, which carves through the terrain, alongside extensive oak forests and agricultural lands surrounding villages like Almanza and Sahagún. The elevation changes are generally gentle, making many routes accessible. Best hiking trails around Cea The most popular hiking route is Senda de los Rebollares (blue route) – circular from Almanza, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a distinctive oak forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bosque de los Enanitos y laguna de la Cota – circular por Almanza, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This trail features a unique forest and a small lagoon near Almanza. Local hikers also love the Almanza — round trip to the Molino Pobladura, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past a historic mill, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking around Cea is defined by river valleys, ancient oak woodlands, and paths connecting historical villages. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 22 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cea?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails documented around Cea, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, providing valuable insights.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Cea?
A: Hikes around Cea typically feature diverse terrain, including paths through extensive oak forests, along the banks of the río Cea, and across agricultural lands. The elevation changes are generally gentle, making many routes accessible. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047721860/senda-de-los-rebollares-blue-route-circular-from-almanza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Rebollares&lt;/a&gt; explores a distinctive oak forest, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176660501/grajal-de-campos-from-sahagun-circular-by-the-rio-cea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grajal de Campos from Sahagún&lt;/a&gt; route follows the river.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cea?
A: Yes, Cea offers several family-friendly hiking options, with 7 easy routes available. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176972048/almanza-round-trip-to-the-molino-pobladura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanza — round trip to the Molino Pobladura&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and leads through rural landscapes and past a historic mill, suitable for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Cea?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary by municipality or protected area. It's always best to check local signage or official tourism information for Almanza, Sahagún, or the specific trail you plan to hike to confirm dog-friendly policies and any requirements for leashes.

Q: What are the best times of year to hike in Cea?
A: The best times to hike in Cea are generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but it's advisable to avoid the midday heat. Winter hiking is also possible, though some paths might be muddy or require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there circular walks available in Cea?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Cea are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047721860/senda-de-los-rebollares-blue-route-circular-from-almanza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Rebollares (blue route) – circular from Almanza&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047710364/bosque-de-los-enanitos-y-laguna-de-la-Cota-circular-por-almanza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de los Enanitos y laguna de la Cota – circular por Almanza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: The Cea region is rich in history and culture. Near the trails, you can explore landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/916337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geographical Center of the Camino Francés – Ermita de la Virgen del Puente&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4441511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Benito Arch and Ruins of the Monastery of San Benito&lt;/a&gt;, and the traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas de Moratinos&lt;/a&gt;. These sites offer a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Cea?
A: While many routes in Cea are easy to moderate, there is at least one difficult trail for those seeking a greater challenge. The region's diverse landscape ensures that hikers of all experience levels can find a suitable route.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Cea?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the trails, such as Almanza and Sahagún. Specific parking information for each route can often be found in the detailed tour descriptions on komoot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cea?
A: The hiking experience in Cea is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 22 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil beauty of the oak woodlands, the peaceful river valleys, and the charm of connecting historical villages. The accessibility of many routes and the well-maintained paths are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails in Cea?
A: Yes, the villages of Almanza and Sahagún, which are common starting points for many trails, offer cafes, pubs, and other establishments where you can find refreshments and meals before or after your hike. These villages provide opportunities to experience local cuisine and hospitality.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails around Cea using public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach the hiking trails around Cea may be limited, especially for more remote trailheads. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for services connecting to villages like Almanza and Sahagún. For greater flexibility, traveling by car is often recommended.
tips: Sahelices del Río is a small town north of Cea in the Cea river valley, hence its name.

The ruins of the castle sit on a hill above the city of Cea. You can go up the hill to see the remains of part of the tower and the entrance arch. Access is free. Fantastic views of the city below and the surrounding grain fields.
